ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-16-2023 Off Calendar

Plaintiff 555 Sutter Gps Summary Of Case, Interest Computation, And Request For Attorneys Fees Pursuant To Cal. Rule Of Court 3.1800 And In Support Of Request For Entry Of Court Default Judgment Against Defendants Lennotch Taplett And Details Barbershop And Executive Grooming Lounge At Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Mar-01-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for request for entry of default judgment and attorneys fees prove-up hearing.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Court Reporter: Kevin Roldan, CSR #13463, kevinaroldan@yahoo.com, 951-283-1337

Plaintiff 555 Sutter GP represented by Johnny J. Yeh, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Lennotch Taplett, Individually and DBA Details Barbsershop and Executive Grooming Lounge not appearing.

CINDY NGUYEN is sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff.

Court is in receipt of supplemental filings made on Feb-22-2023.
Court has reviewed the case and all filings pertaining to this hearing.

The following exhibits are MARKED/ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE:

Plaintiff's Exhibit No. 1 - Customer Balance Detail

Court examines CINDY NGUYEN.
Counsel for Plaintiff presents outline, summary of case, an offer of proof, and is examined by the Court.

Court finds that liability is not at question.
Plaintiff's request to be awarded rent for the period of Dec-04-2020 to Jan-27-2021 is argued and DENIED.

Court finds that the State of California suspended Barber and Hair Services for the period of Dec-04-2020 to Jan-27-2021. The business at issue was not allowed to operate during this time due to COVID.

Court hereby deducts amount for rent, common facitlity fees, and security deposit as follows:

1. The rate of $79.33 a Day for 27 days in December of the year 2020 for a total deduction of $2,141.91

2. The rate of $76.77 a Day for 27 days in January of the year 2021 for a total deduction of $2,072.79

3. Operating Costs for 2 months for a total deduction of $957.44

4. Security Deposit for a total deduction of $7,140.00

TOTAL DEDUCTION: $12,312.14
Plaintiff's request for damages in the sum of $47,436.87 - $12,312.14 = $35,124.73 in Damages

Counsel for Plaintiff examined re: it declaration for attorney fees.

The matter is submitted.
Based on the summary of the case, comments made by Plaintiff's witness and its Counsel, and evidence presented, the Court finds that Plaintiff has proven each essential element of the complaint. Defendant failed to pay rent beginning October 2020 to the time they vacated the premises. The Court further considered a 54 day deduction during the suspension of services.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff 555 Sutter GP, and against Defendant Lennotch Taplett, Individually and DBA Details Barbsershop and Executive Grooming Lounge, as follows:

1. Damages: $35,124.73
2. Attorney Fees: $7,899.50
3. Costs: $2,344.78

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $45,369.01

Proposed judgment signed as modified.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

Plaintiff 555 Sutter Gps Summary Of Case, Interest Computation, And Request For Attorneys Fees Pursuant To Cal. Off Calendar Jan-17-2023 Continued To Mar-01-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 505
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for request for entry of court default judgment.

Dept. 514; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Court Reporter: Kevin Roldan, CSR #13463, 951-283-1337, kevinaroldan@yahoo.com

Plaintiff 555 Sutter GP represented by Johnny J. Yeh, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Lennotch Taplett, Individually and Doing Business as Details Barbershop and Executive Grooming Lounge not appearing.

CINDY NGUYEN is sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff.
Counsel for Plaintiff presents case summary and an offer of proof.

The following exhibits are MARKED/ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE:

Plaintiff's Exhibit No. 1 - Multi-Tenant Retail Lease for 555 Sutter Street, San Francisco, CA 94102

Court inquires about an accounting as to what monthly rents were paid or not paid.
Counsel for Plaintiff shall file supplemental briefing to support its request. Briefing must include an accounting as to what monthly rents were paid or not paid, disclose any funds applied to outstanding balance, and indicate when beauty services were allowed to resume during the pandemic by Feb-22-2023.

Plaintiff's Application for an Order Waiving Witness Requirement and for Permission to Proceed to Court Default Judgment filed Jan-03-2023 is DENIED.

Court orders matter continued to Mar-01-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 505 for further hearing on Plaintiff's request for entry court default judgment.
